posts.text: nanoGPT v2 is out. Added flash attention, gradient checkpointing, and a cleaner training loop. ~40% faster on A100. Still fits on a single GPU for experiments.
posts.summary: nanoGPT v2 ships with flash attention and 40% training speed improvement on A100.
posts.whyItMatters: A widely-used educational LLM baseline just got significantly faster — relevant for anyone running small-scale experiments.

posts.text: The benchmark race is getting silly. Models optimizing for MMLU while failing on basic spatial reasoning is a pattern the community keeps ignoring. Capability fragmentation is real.
posts.summary: Argues that benchmark-optimized models mask genuine reasoning gaps, especially in spatial tasks.
posts.whyItMatters: Raises a persistent critique of how the field measures progress.

posts.text: Just finished writing a 3000-word explainer on how attention mechanisms actually work in modern LLMs. The intuition is simpler than most papers make it seem. Posting as a thread.
posts.summary: Long-form explainer thread on attention mechanisms aimed at demystifying the core LLM building block.

posts.text: GPT-5 context window is now 1M tokens in the API for all paid tiers. Writing code that touches your entire codebase in one call is now real. Pricing stays the same.
posts.summary: GPT-5 gets a 1M-token context window across all paid API tiers at unchanged pricing.
posts.whyItMatters: 1M context at current price points fundamentally changes what's practical for code and document analysis.

posts.text: Safety and capability are not at odds. We've been publishing alignment research alongside capability work for years. Today's paper on scalable oversight adds another data point.
posts.summary: Argues alignment and capability research are complementary, citing a new scalable oversight paper.

posts.text: Gemini 2.5 Pro scored above human expert level on 12 new graduate-level chemistry benchmarks. This is a meaningful science milestone — not just a benchmark number.
posts.summary: Gemini 2.5 Pro achieves above-human-expert performance on 12 graduate-level chemistry benchmarks.
posts.whyItMatters: Scientific domain competence at expert level has direct implications for drug discovery and materials research.

posts.text: Applying AlphaFold-style structural prediction to materials science. Early results: crystal stability prediction 2x better than DFT at 1/100th the compute. Full paper next month.
posts.summary: Google DeepMind adapts AlphaFold's approach to materials science with promising crystal stability results.
posts.whyItMatters: If it holds up, this approach could dramatically accelerate materials discovery for batteries, semiconductors, and clean energy.

posts.text: Open source AI is not a security risk. Closed models with unverifiable safety claims are riskier. The safety argument for closing source code has always been weak and self-serving.
posts.summary: Makes the case that open-source AI is safer than closed models with opaque safety claims.

posts.text: JEPA: new results on self-supervised video understanding. No language supervision required — just patch-level prediction. State-of-the-art on Kinetics-700 without fine-tuning.
posts.summary: New JEPA results show state-of-the-art video understanding via self-supervised patch prediction, no language needed.

posts.text: New Coursera specialization on LLM fine-tuning is live. 4 courses, project-based, includes LoRA, QLoRA, and RLHF labs. Certificate track. Accessible on all plan tiers.
posts.summary: New Coursera LLM fine-tuning specialization launches with 4 hands-on courses covering LoRA, QLoRA, and RLHF.

posts.text: AI Fund portfolio company just closed $12M Series A for LLM-native contract review for SMBs. Lawyers using AI tools are 3x more productive. The market is real.
posts.summary: AI Fund-backed legal-tech startup raises $12M for LLM-native contract review targeting small firms.

posts.text: o3-mini is now available in the API. 40% cheaper than o3, same reasoning quality on most coding and math benchmarks. Rate limits are 2x higher at launch.
posts.summary: o3-mini launches in the API at 40% lower cost than o3 with doubled rate limits.
posts.whyItMatters: Cost reduction on the best reasoning model makes it practical for higher-volume applications.

posts.text: Structured outputs v2: stricter JSON mode, better array handling, first-class enum support, and nullable fields. Breaking change in the return type — check the migration guide.
posts.summary: Structured Outputs v2 ships with better JSON schema compliance and nullable field support.

posts.text: Recording a 5-hour conversation with Ilya Sutskever tomorrow. Topics: scaling laws, alignment, what happened at OpenAI, consciousness, and where he's going next.
posts.summary: Announces a 5-hour Ilya Sutskever interview covering scaling laws, alignment, and his post-OpenAI plans.
posts.whyItMatters: Sutskever's first extended public interview since leaving OpenAI will likely move conversations in the field.

posts.text: Spatial intelligence in machines is the next frontier. Vision + language alone is not enough. We need embodied reasoning grounded in 3D space. New HAI paper outlines the research agenda.
posts.summary: Argues that embodied 3D spatial reasoning is the next major milestone for AI, citing new HAI research.

posts.text: fast.ai lesson 5 is live: backprop from scratch, no frameworks, just Python and numpy. If you can't explain it line by line, you can't debug it when it matters.
posts.summary: fast.ai lesson 5 covers backpropagation from scratch in pure Python, reinforcing fundamental ML intuition.

posts.text: Benchmarked 8 open models on multi-step instruction following and structured JSON output tasks. Llama 3.1 70B is surprisingly strong. Qwen2.5-72B wins on complex nested JSON. Thread with full numbers.
posts.summary: Detailed benchmark comparing 8 open models on instruction-following and structured output tasks.

posts.text: Tutorial: fine-tune Mistral 7B on a medical Q&A dataset with QLoRA on a single A10. Under 2 hours, under $8. Full Jupyter notebook with all config values included.
posts.summary: Step-by-step QLoRA fine-tuning tutorial for Mistral 7B on medical Q&A, under $8 on a single A10.

posts.text: 82% of Fortune 500 have at least one production AI system in 2025. The enterprise AI wave is real and largely silent. Most aren't talking about it publicly because it's competitive.
posts.summary: 82% of Fortune 500 have production AI deployments, though most remain quiet about it for competitive reasons.

posts.text: Built an agent that generates, tests, and deploys microservices end-to-end from a single prompt. Not perfect, but ships working code 70% of the time on first try. Demo thread.
posts.summary: Demonstrates an agent that autonomously generates, tests, and deploys microservices from a single prompt at 70% success rate.
posts.whyItMatters: Agentic code deployment hitting 70% reliability on first try is a meaningful practical threshold.

posts.text: Reflection-Llama-3.1-70B beats GPT-4o on 6 of 8 reasoning benchmarks. Trained with self-reflection fine-tuning. Open-sourcing weights and training recipe today.
posts.summary: Open-sources Reflection-Llama-3.1-70B, which beats GPT-4o on most reasoning benchmarks via self-reflection training.

posts.text: OpenAI Operator mode can now fill forms, book appointments, and navigate any website in the browser. It asks before consequential actions. This is the beginning of the agent layer.
posts.summary: OpenAI Operator ships browser-level agency including form filling, booking, and navigation with consent checkpoints.
posts.whyItMatters: Browser-native agents with consent gates marks the practical start of the agentic automation era.

posts.text: This week's AI paper digest: 14 papers. Highlights: mixture-of-experts scaling laws, RLHF alternatives, two long-context retrieval papers, and a surprising result on small model reasoning.
posts.summary: Weekly roundup covers MoE scaling, RLHF alternatives, long-context retrieval, and small-model reasoning results.

posts.text: The AI wrapper business is not dead. It's just filtering. Winners own distribution or data moats. Losers bet on the model. Thread on what's actually working right now.
posts.summary: Argues AI wrappers still work if they own distribution or proprietary data, not just model access.

posts.text: Claude Code just became my primary IDE. Rewrote a 2000-line Swift module overnight and all tests pass. I didn't write a single line of boilerplate. Screenshot in thread.
posts.summary: Reports using Claude Code to fully rewrite a 2000-line Swift module overnight with all tests passing.

posts.text: Tested 4 AI code completion tools head-to-head on my real codebase: Copilot, Cursor, Codeium, Claude Code. Different strengths. Full write-up thread.
posts.summary: Head-to-head comparison of Copilot, Cursor, Codeium, and Claude Code on a real production codebase.

posts.text: Shipped: AI feature builder for product teams. Give it a Figma frame + feature description, get a working React component with tests. Still rough but it ships real code.
posts.summary: Ships a tool that converts Figma frames + descriptions into working React components with tests.

posts.text: Agentic coding loops: what breaks most often based on 200 hours of running unsupervised agents. The answer is context management, not capability. Here's what I learned.
posts.summary: Shares findings from 200 hours of unsupervised agent runs: context management is the primary failure mode, not model capability.
posts.whyItMatters: Practical failure analysis from extended real-world agent runs is rare and genuinely useful.

posts.text: I replaced my entire Notion workflow with a Claude prompt that reads my notes and outputs structured project plans. Exact prompt and setup in thread.
posts.summary: Shares a prompt-based workflow replacing Notion with Claude for structured project planning.

posts.text: Nomad List crossed 500k registered users. No team. AI handles 70% of support, 40% of the feature roadmap. Total infrastructure cost: $2400/month. Numbers thread.
posts.summary: Nomad List reaches 500k users as a one-person operation with AI handling most support and roadmap decisions.
posts.whyItMatters: Concrete numbers on solo-founder AI leverage at scale are rare and instructive.

posts.text: My AI tools stack in 2025: Claude for writing and code, Midjourney v7 for images, Suno for marketing audio. Total monthly cost: $140. Revenue from AI-assisted products: $400k/month.
posts.summary: Shares a $140/month AI tools stack generating $400k/month in revenue across AI-assisted products.

posts.text: Month 3 update: $23k MRR across 4 AI products, all built solo with AI assistance. The compounding is real once you stop doing the boring parts yourself.
posts.summary: Reports $23k MRR from 4 solo-built AI products after 3 months, emphasizing compounding output with AI assistance.

posts.text: Testing video-to-code: give Claude a screen recording and it outputs a working UI component. Hit rate around 65% on complex layouts. Sharing results and failure cases.
posts.summary: Experiments with video-to-UI-code using Claude; reports 65% success on complex layouts with analysis of failures.

posts.text: Agent that monitors GitHub issues and auto-drafts responses based on your repo's past answers. Scarily good at matching your voice. Open-sourcing next week.
posts.summary: Building an agent that monitors GitHub issues and drafts responses by learning from past answer patterns.

posts.text: Chain-of-density prompting: get 5x more information density in AI summaries through iterative compression. Full guide with before/after comparisons and reusable template.
posts.summary: Publishes a guide on chain-of-density prompting for higher-information summaries with reusable templates.

posts.text: New prompt pattern: add 'assume the role of a skeptical reviewer' before any analysis task. Dramatically reduces hallucination and overconfidence. Works across Claude, GPT-4, Gemini.
posts.summary: Shares a 'skeptical reviewer' prompt pattern that reduces hallucination across major models.

posts.text: Multi-agent orchestration patterns after building 20+ production systems: fork-join, sequential, and debate patterns each have specific failure modes. When to use which. Thread.
posts.summary: Breaks down multi-agent orchestration patterns from production experience, covering failure modes of fork-join, sequential, and debate approaches.
posts.whyItMatters: Production-tested agent orchestration patterns from someone who's built many are hard to find.

posts.text: RAG is not enough for large codebases. You need a code graph + semantic chunking + query decomposition. Here's the full stack that actually works in production.
posts.summary: Argues that production RAG for large codebases requires code graphs, semantic chunking, and query decomposition beyond naive vector search.

posts.text: Built an AI that watches your Zoom calls and auto-writes follow-up emails with action items. Weekend project. Claude + Whisper. The output quality surprised me. Code below.
posts.summary: Demos a Claude + Whisper pipeline that generates Zoom follow-up emails with action items automatically.

posts.text: AI-generated ad creatives outperform human-made ones on 7 of 8 tested ad accounts. The creative agency model is being disrupted faster than most people in the industry admit.
posts.summary: AI ad creatives outperform human creatives on most tested accounts, signaling accelerating disruption of creative agencies.

posts.text: AI + Figma workflow: using Claude to generate component variants, write accessibility annotations, and draft design specs from existing components. 3x faster on design handoff.
posts.summary: Demonstrates using Claude inside a Figma workflow to generate variants, annotations, and specs at 3x handoff speed.

posts.text: Built a system that reads a brand guide PDF and outputs a complete Tailwind config, component library stubs, and color palette. Open beta this week.
posts.summary: Open-betas a tool that converts brand guide PDFs into Tailwind configs and component library stubs.

posts.text: AI for climate: trained a model on 60 years of satellite imagery to predict urban heat islands with 94% accuracy. First deployment in 5 cities starting next month.
posts.summary: Announces deployment of an AI urban heat island prediction model trained on 60 years of satellite data.

posts.text: Scaling is not dead. We're still on the curve. The question is where the curve flattens and we don't have definitive evidence yet. The pessimists are front-running uncertainty.
posts.summary: Pushes back on scaling skepticism, arguing there's no definitive evidence the scaling curve has flattened.

posts.text: Shipped a feature today that would have taken me 3 days last year. AI is not killing dev jobs. It's killing the excuses not to ship.
posts.summary: Reflects on AI-accelerated shipping speed, reframing the jobs debate around removing friction.
